allow_growth.reason: Dependency growth is never allowed under the stdlib-only rule. Any positive delta must emit an overflow finding. Zero tolerance for new runtime dependencies.

SP14 (MPRR) adds its orchestration docs to repo-A: the design spec, the implementation plan, the launch prompt, the SP15 freeze stub, and the per-iteration MPRR run ledger. The engine code + tests live in repo-B (not counted here). The ledger is appended once per Phase-B iteration (hard cap 12), so docs-LOC grows steadily through the run; this allowance covers the full SP14 documentation footprint.
allow_growth.expires: next repo-A release after SP14 closeout (re-baseline + purge, mirroring the v0.6.0 SP13 purge)

allow_growth.reason: repo-A's SP14 changes are documentation-only (engine code is in repo-B), so net-LOC growth tracks docs-LOC growth: spec + plan + launch prompt + SP15 stub + the per-iteration ledger appends. Same release-expiring lifecycle as docs_loc_growth.
allow_growth.expires: next repo-A release after SP14 closeout (re-baseline + purge, mirroring the v0.6.0 SP13 purge)

allow_growth.reason: SP14 adds a small fixed set of new tracked doc files to repo-A (spec, plan, launch-prompt split, ledger, SP15-CANDIDATES stub). Phase B appends to the single ledger file rather than adding files; the small headroom covers any incidental orchestration artifact.
allow_growth.expires: next repo-A release after SP14 closeout (re-baseline + purge, mirroring the v0.6.0 SP13 purge)

allow_growth.reason: The MPRR engine's CLI flags (mprr_run plan/integrate/reaudit: --run-dir, --findings, --triage, --ceiling, --repo, --packet-id, --evidence, --diff-files, --branch, --no-merge) are documented in repo-A's plan and launch prompt; the detector counts them as new flag declarations. The engine ships in repo-B; this is documentation surface, and the flag set is fixed for SP14.
allow_growth.expires: next repo-A release after SP14 closeout (re-baseline + purge, mirroring the v0.6.0 SP13 purge)
